stimulus.content:
Read the excerpt below:

The Constitution begins with the words "We the People." The founders chose these words to show that the government gets its power from the citizens of the country.

stem: Why did the founders begin the Constitution with the words "We the People"?

choices.text: To show that only leaders could make laws
To show that government power comes from citizens
To list the names of all the founders
To explain how judges are chosen

answer_explanation: The words 'We the People' show that the government gets its power from the people. The phrase does not limit lawmaking to leaders, list names of founders, or explain how judges are selected.
